title: centaurea aplolepa subsp. bertolonii
intro: Centaurea aplolepa subsp. bertolonii, also known as Bertoloni's knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea aplolepa plant, which is a flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Centaurea aplolepa subsp. bertolonii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
